hero.subtitle: Onvanta was born out of frustration. After 15+ years in print-on-demand, I watched good people fail — not because they weren't capable, but because nobody gave them a proper start.

stories.title: The problem we kept seeing
stories.p1: Customer service reps telling clients that pad printing can handle full-colour photos. Sales reps quoting screen printing without counting logo colours. Operators guessing procedures because the knowledge lived in one person's head.
stories.p2: The problem wasn't the people. It was the onboarding. Or rather: the lack of it. A Word document, a day shadowing someone, and a "you'll figure it out" — that's what most teams were working with.

stories.title: Why we built Onvanta
stories.p1: We wanted something that combines structured onboarding with real knowledge retention. Not just "here's a checklist" — but a system that ensures knowledge actually sticks, using spaced repetition the way language apps do.
stories.p2: And we wanted managers to have real visibility. Not a 30-day check-in where you discover someone has been struggling for three weeks. Real-time signals so you can step in early.

stories.title: Who we're building for
stories.p1: Right now: print-on-demand and logistics companies with 10–50 employees. We know this world from the inside and our templates reflect that — real print technique knowledge, real customer communication scenarios, real procedures.
stories.p2: Coming soon: any growing business that hires people and wants to make them productive faster. The problem is universal. The solution scales with it.

items.title: Specificity beats generality
items.desc: A template built for POD customer service is worth more than a generic onboarding checklist. We build for specific roles, specific industries.

items.title: Knowledge must stick
items.desc: Reading something once isn't learning. Spaced repetition isn't a luxury — it's the difference between knowing and forgetting.

items.title: Managers need signals, not reports
items.desc: A 30-day review is too late. Managers need to know in week one if someone is struggling — so they can still do something about it.

cta.title: Ready for smarter onboarding?
cta.subtitle: 14 days free. Full access. No credit card required.
